item.title: Trump Tells G7 Iran 'About to Surrender' — Tehran Flatly Rejects; White House Fractures Over War Exit; UK: Putin's Hand Behind Iran Drones
item.description:
The Iran war enters a new phase of internal contradiction: Trump privately told G7 allies Iran is about to fold, Iran's foreign minister publicly called it fiction, and a Reuters exclusive reveals White House factions brawling over when and how to declare victory.

Trump to G7: Iran Is "About to Surrender" — Iran: Absolutely Not

Trump told leaders from the Group of Seven in a virtual call on March 11 that Iran is "about to surrender," Axios reported March 13, citing three officials from G7 countries on the call. Trump boasted about Operation Epic Fury's results: "got rid of a cancer that was threatening us all." He also acknowledged a critical problem: "nobody knows who is the leader, so there is no one who can announce surrender." At a Kentucky rally the same day, Trump declared "we won" — then walked it back hours later.

Iranian Foreign Minister Syed Abbas Araghchi rejected the claim flatly: Iran is "not approaching the Americans for diplomatic negotiations or a ceasefire." Accountability, he said, lies entirely with the US and Israel. Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei reiterated his vow to avenge civilian deaths. Large crowds of Iranians turned out in Tehran and other cities in unsolicited pro-government demonstrations — a visible show of public support that complicates the US narrative of a regime on the verge of collapse.

White House Tug-of-War: Three Factions, No Clear Exit (Reuters Exclusive)

A Reuters investigation (March 13, byline Bose/Spetalnick/Pamuk) reveals three competing factions inside the White House fighting for Trump's ear on war strategy:

  • Economic team: Gasoline prices up 65 cents/gallon in one month. Warns that sustained war will exact major political cost ahead of midterms.
  • Political aides: Push for a quick, limited-operation narrative — declare objectives met, wind down before the damage compounds.
  • Hawks: Argue that Iran's regime must be permanently degraded; any premature exit leaves the job unfinished and emboldens adversaries.

The result is Trump's whipsawing public statements — "we won" followed by fresh escalation warnings. Reuters calls it "the biggest US military operation since the 2003 Iraq war" and notes no exit path has been agreed internally.

UK: Putin's "Hidden Hand" Is Coaching Iran's Drone Tactics

UK Defence Secretary John Healey told reporters March 12 that Putin's "hidden hand" is likely behind some of Iran's drone attack tactics — and possibly capabilities. British Chief of Joint Operations Lt. Gen. Nick Perry said there are "definitively" signs of a Russia-Iran link, including Iran deploying drones "as learned from the Russians." The UK is analyzing the drone that struck RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus on March 1 for Russian components. The circular logic: Iran supplied Russia with Shahed-136 kamikaze drones for Ukraine; Russia is now returning the favor with electronic warfare and drone swarm tactics refined on the Ukrainian battlefield. Trump, asked separately, said he had "no indication" Russia was helping Iran but that if they were, it wasn't "helping much."

Hormuz: Selective Passage Emerging

Iran allowing select tankers through Hormuz for favored nations — India and Turkey both cleared vessels this week. The IRGC permission system appears to be functioning as a geopolitical lever: reward cooperation, punish US/EU/Israeli-aligned shipping. Hegseth said in a briefing that the US has "no evidence" Iran has formally mined the Strait (mining warehouses and capability remain confirmed; operational deployment is unconfirmed). Brent remains above $100. US-Russia oil sanctions waiver (until April 11) for vessels already at sea is aimed at cushioning the price spike.

NemoClaw Confirmed Shipping Today at GTC — With New Mascot "Nivi"

NVIDIA's enterprise AI agent platform NemoClaw ships today at GTC 2026. Jensen Huang is expected to formally introduce the platform at the 11am PT keynote. Alongside the technical launch, NVIDIA is introducing "Nivi" — a digital mascot designed as a "companion spirit for the age of agentic AI." NVIDIA VP Manuvir Das: "As AI begins to execute critical, multi-step business tasks autonomously, trust and clarity are paramount." NemoClaw runs on NVIDIA NIM (Inference Microservices) but is hardware-agnostic — AMD and Intel hardware supported. Enterprise partnerships in discussion: Salesforce, Cisco, Google, Adobe, CrowdStrike (none confirmed). Positioned explicitly as the enterprise alternative to OpenClaw (acquired by OpenAI in February).

item.title: GTC 2026 Keynote Day: NVIDIA + Thinking Machines Lab Gigawatt Deal Confirmed; Jensen Huang Takes Stage
item.description:
GTC 2026 opens in San Jose today as Jensen Huang delivers the most-watched AI hardware keynote of the year — and NVIDIA's first confirmed gigawatt-scale Vera Rubin customer is already on the board before he walks onstage.

NVIDIA + Thinking Machines Lab: First Gigawatt-Scale Vera Rubin Customer

NVIDIA and Thinking Machines Lab — the AI company founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati — announced a multiyear strategic partnership to deploy at least one gigawatt of next-generation NVIDIA Vera Rubin systems for frontier model training. NVIDIA made a significant equity investment in Thinking Machines as part of the deal, targeting deployment as early as 2027. Jensen Huang: "world-class team." Murati: "NVIDIA is the foundation on which the entire field is built." This is the first confirmed major Vera Rubin customer deal at gigawatt scale — announced March 10, before GTC opened.

GTC 2026 Pre-Show (8am PT): Industry Leaders on the AI Infrastructure Build-Out

Hosted by Sarah Guo (Conviction), Gavin Baker (Atreides Management), and Alfred Lin (Sequoia Capital), the GTC Live pre-show runs three hours ahead of Jensen's keynote. Key guests across five tracks:

  • Accelerated computing: Palantir President Aki Jain, Cadence CEO Anirudh Devgan, IBM's Dinesh Nirmal, Morgan Stanley's Mark Edelstone
  • AI infrastructure: Dell CEO Michael Dell, CoreWeave CEO Michael Intrator, Caterpillar CEO Joe Creed, Fireworks AI CEO Lin Qiao
  • Open models: Cohere CEO Aidan Gomez, Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas, Mistral CEO Arthur Mensch, Black Forest Labs CEO Robin Rombach
  • Agentic AI: LangChain CEO Harrison Chase, OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger, PrimeIntellect CEO Vincent Weisser, Edison Scientific CEO Samuel Rodriques
  • Physical AI: Waabi CEO Raquel Urtasun, SkildAI CEO Deepak Pathak, PhysicsX CEO Jacomo Corbo

Jensen Keynote (11am PT): Expected Announcements

30,000 in-person at SAP Center (San Jose Sharks arena); 300,000+ livestreaming. Jensen's two-hour keynote covers the full stack — chips, software, models, applications. Key expected reveals: Vera Rubin (10x inference cost reduction vs. Blackwell), Rubin CPX (1M-token context, enough to read an entire codebase in one shot), Feynman (first public reveal on TSMC A16 1.6nm with Silicon Photonics — NVIDIA reportedly TSMC's first and only early A16 customer), and N1X AI PC Superchip. OpenClaw "Build-a-Claw" event runs GTC Park March 16–19.

Jensen to Moderate Open Models Panel (March 18)

On Wednesday March 18 at 12:30pm PT, Jensen will personally moderate a panel on open models vs. frontier closed models with LangChain's Harrison Chase, plus leaders from A16Z, AI2, Cursor, and Thinking Machines Lab — cementing Murati's presence at the center of NVIDIA's open-model narrative.

Iran-US War: Day 17 — Overnight Quiet, Barzin Inbound

No major new anchor events overnight. Barzin (China-flagged, suspected sodium perchlorate) remains inbound to Bandar Abbas with ETA March 20 — no US interdiction signaled. Iran's three ceasefire conditions (recognition, reparations, binding guarantees) remain in place; no negotiations confirmed. US strategic recalibration continues after Reuters report (3 sources) that the Iranian regime is "not in danger of collapse." Brent crude remains above $100. De-mining of Hormuz estimated weeks-to-months even if ceasefire announced.

item.title: US Intel: Iran Regime Not Collapsing; Netanyahu Drops Regime Change Goal; FBI: Iran Eyed California Drone Strike
item.description:
Iran–US War: Day 17 Midnight Update — March 16, 2026

US Intelligence: Iran Regime "Not in Danger" of Collapse

A bombshell Reuters report (March 12-13), confirmed by three sources familiar with US intelligence findings, directly contradicts Washington's stated war objectives: a "multitude" of intelligence assessments conclude the Iranian regime is "not in danger" of collapse and "retains control of the Iranian public." The most recent assessment was completed within the last few days. The findings pose the central strategic question of the war: after 17 days of Operation Epic Fury, killing Supreme Leader Khamenei, striking 15,000+ targets, and spending over $11 billion — what exactly is the endgame?

Democratic senators who received a classified briefing from Trump administration officials emerged saying the US has "no plan" in Iran. Previous CIA assessments reportedly concluded that removing Iran's leaders could cause "an even more radical group" to emerge. Meanwhile, Trump told CBS News the war will end "soon, very soon" — a signal that political pressure to exit is building, even without a decisive outcome.

Netanyahu Abandons Regime Change Goal

In his first press conference since the war began, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu made a notable retreat: "I do not deny it — I cannot say with certainty that the Iranian people will bring down the regime." He added: "A regime is ultimately brought down from within." An Israeli security official told Channel 12: "The chance to overthrow the Iranian regime, which could have gone down in history, now seems less realistic." The new framing from Israeli military and political leaders is shifting from regime change to "changing the Middle East" — a lower bar. This represents a significant strategic recalibration from Israel's opening position that the war would replace the clerical regime entirely.

Netanyahu wants Iran to "create conditions for regime change" — but without the certainty it will succeed, the US and Israel face the prospect of a costly, prolonged conflict against a regime the intelligence community says is holding.

FBI Alert: Iran Eyed Ship-Launched Drone Strike on California

The FBI distributed an alert to California law enforcement warning of "unverified information" that Iran had "allegedly aspired" to launch a surprise drone attack on California from an unidentified vessel off the US coast, targeting unspecified California locations, in the event the US struck Iran. The information was dated from early February — before the war began. California Governor Newsom acknowledged awareness of the threat but said there is no imminent threat. The FBI alert: "We have no additional information on the timing, method, target, or perpetrators." Trump dismissed the concern, telling reporters: "No, I'm not [concerned]." Camp Pendleton confirmed receipt of the alert. The warning is unverified, but the FBI's decision to distribute it signals active concern about Iran escalating to US soil.

IDF Opens New Raid Wave on Day 17

The Israeli army has begun a new series of raids against Iran overnight, per liveuamap.com. Operation Epic Fury continues its daily tempo. Strikes continue across multiple provinces. Air defenses activated in Tehran overnight. 20+ strike waves per day remains the operational pace; IDF has now completed "hundreds of strike waves against the regime" over 17 days (Jerusalem Post). CENTCOM continues to target Iranian naval mine supply chain.

Netanyahu: "Create Conditions" for Iran Regime Change — GTC Keynote Today

Separately, Netanyahu told Iranians in a March 11 X post: "Your dreams will become a reality. When the time is right, and that time is fast approaching, we will pass the torch to you. Be ready to seize the moment!" The tension between these public statements and the private intelligence picture is stark.

GTC keynote today at 11am PT — Jensen Huang takes the SAP Center stage in San Jose in ~19 hours. 300,000+ watching live. "Several new chips the world has never seen before." Full coverage in the next dedicated update after the keynote.

item.title: First French Soldier Killed in Erbil; Dubai DIFC Hit; GTC Keynote Eve — Jensen's "World-Surprising" Chip Tomorrow
item.description:
Iran–US War: Day 16 Evening Update — March 15, 2026

First French Death: CWO Arnaud Frion Killed in Erbil Drone Strike

French President Emmanuel Macron confirmed the first French military fatality of the war: Chief Warrant Officer Arnaud Frion of the 7th Chasseurs Alpins Battalion was killed in a drone strike on a counterterrorism training base at Mala Qara, ~40km from Erbil in Iraqi Kurdistan. Several other soldiers were wounded. The strike involved two drones and was claimed by Islamic Resistance in Iraq (Ashab Alkahf faction), which had pre-warned that French interests were now targets after a French carrier arrived in CENTCOM's operational area. Macron: "Their presence in Iraq is strictly within the framework of the fight against terrorism. The war in Iran cannot justify such attacks." France has stationed troops in the region since 2015 as part of the anti-ISIS coalition. This is the first NATO-member fatality of the war and marks a significant political escalation — Italy withdrew from Erbil days earlier, but France stayed. Iraq is now a confirmed second battlefield, with pro-Iranian groups targeting all foreign military presence.

Dubai Financial District Struck — Black Smoke Near Burj Khalifa

A kamikaze drone breached UAE air defenses over central Dubai on March 13, with debris from an intercepted projectile striking a building in the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC) — one of the world's premier financial hubs, located steps from the Burj Khalifa. Black smoke was visible over the financial district. UAE authorities confirmed the interception but acknowledged debris reached a building in the Al Quoz area. "An explosion at the Dubai International Financial Centre, right next to my building," one witness wrote on X. This comes after UAE air defenses had already intercepted 10 ballistic missiles and 26 drones in preceding days. Financial markets traders in DIFC — home to hundreds of hedge funds, banks and asset managers — reported buildings shaking. The strike signals Iran's willingness to target global financial infrastructure.

Axis of Resistance: First Simultaneous Hezbollah-IRGC Barrage; Iraqi Militia Surge

Reuters and AL-Monitor confirm Hezbollah and Iran's IRGC conducted their first-ever simultaneous coordinated rocket barrage on Israel — Hezbollah firing 200 rockets in coordination with IRGC operations. Israeli defenses intercepted all but two. The joint execution demonstrates a new level of operational coordination across the Axis of Resistance. Separately, Iraqi Shi'a militias — including Kata'ib Hezbollah and Harakat al-Nujaba — have significantly stepped up drone and missile attacks on US interests in Iraq over the past 3-4 days. CENTCOM has responded with targeted strikes on militia command infrastructure in Iraq. The Axis, though weakened by 16 days of strikes, is demonstrating it can sustain and even accelerate multi-front operations.

Iran Targets Turkish Base Hosting US Troops

Video verified by Al Jazeera shows a projectile landing near a Turkish military base in northern Iraq that also hosts US troops. No casualties have been reported but the incident expands Iran's targeting to NATO-member military infrastructure. Turkey has not formally entered the conflict and maintains back-channel communication with Tehran.

GTC Eve: Jensen's "World-Surprising Chip" — Keynote Tomorrow 11am PT

NVIDIA GTC 2026 opens tomorrow. Jensen Huang delivers the keynote at 11am PT (2pm ET) at the SAP Center, San Jose — 30,000 in person, 300,000+ livestream at nvidia.com/gtc. NVIDIA's market cap is ~$4.6 trillion, making this "the Super Bowl of AI" with more financial stakes than any prior GTC.

What Jensen has teased: "Several new chips the world has never seen before." Industry consensus is the "surprise" is a dual-pronged move into consumer AI and infrastructure: the N1X AI PC Superchip — co-developed with a partner, built with Silicon Photonics for hybrid on-device/cloud inference — alongside the confirmed Vera Rubin GPU (10x inference cost reduction vs. current gen), Rubin CPX (1M-token context), and the first architectural look at Feynman (TSMC A16 1.6nm, purpose-built for AI agent long-term memory and reasoning). NemoClaw — NVIDIA's open-source enterprise AI agent platform — formally launches tomorrow. At GTC Park (March 16-19, 8am-5pm), attendees can "Build-a-Claw" — customizing and deploying personal AI agents on-site using OpenClaw, with NVIDIA DGX Spark hardware available to buy.

War Economy: Barzin Still En Route, Dubai Tankers Frozen

The second Chinese rocket-fuel ship, Barzin, continues toward Bandar Abbas with ETA March 20. No US interdiction action taken on Shabdis (which arrived Chabahar this morning) or Barzin. The US 30-day OFAC license for Russian oil at sea runs through April 11. Brent remains above $100 with the IEA's 400M-barrel release having failed to break the psychological floor. Shipping frozen across the Gulf — 500+ tankers bottled — with tanker owners now citing "act of war" insurance clauses to exit voyages without penalty.

item.title: Shabdis Arrives Chabahar; Mojtaba Vows Revenge; NVIDIA NemoClaw Debuts at GTC Tomorrow
item.description:
Iran–US War: Day 16 Noon Update — March 15, 2026

Shabdis Arrives Chabahar — No Interdiction

The Iranian cargo ship Shabdis, which departed Gaolan Port in Zhuhai, China, has arrived at Chabahar, Iran, as anticipated. The vessel — operated by the sanctioned Islamic Republic of Iran Shipping Lines (IRISL) — is suspected of carrying sodium perchlorate, a key precursor for solid rocket fuel. Carnegie Endowment fellow Isaac Kardon: "Given the track record, the most parsimonious explanation is that they're loading the same commodity they've been shuttling for the past year-plus." The second vessel, Barzin, is on course for Bandar Abbas with ETA March 20. Neither ship has been intercepted despite active US operations. The delivery represents a meaningful resupply of Iran's missile propellant stockpile.

Mojtaba's First Statement: "Avenge the Blood," Close All US Bases

Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first public statement since assuming power, read on-air by an Iranian state TV anchor — Khamenei has not appeared in person or on camera since his appointment. The statement: Iran will not "refrain from avenging the blood of its martyrs"; the Strait of Hormuz blockade must continue; and all US military bases in the Middle East "must close immediately — those bases will be attacked." Trump responded: "I think he probably is [alive] — I think he is damaged." Israeli intelligence says Khamenei was injured in the strike that killed his father. Per CNBC, oil prices extended gains following the comments. Iran has warned Brent could reach $200/bbl.

Iran Cluster Missiles Breach Israeli Defenses — 70 Bomblets Over Central Israel

Haaretz reports 11 Iranian cluster missiles have penetrated Israeli defenses during the 16-day campaign; one dropped at least 70 bomblets over central Israel. No fatalities reported in that strike, but the penetrations are a notable failure for Israel's multi-layer air defense network, which Hegseth claimed is stopping 90%+ of missiles. The Fattah-2 hypersonic (Mach 13-15) has been flagged as a persistent gap in Iron Dome capability.

Lebanon: Israel Prepares Northern Expansion

IDF continues strikes on south Beirut with renewed mass evacuation orders. Israel has instructed ground forces to prepare for further incursions into northern Lebanon, extending beyond the border zones already under Israeli control. Amal Movement's vote to ban Hezbollah military activity continues to deepen the fracture in Hezbollah's domestic coalition. UN report: 120+ rockets fired from Lebanon toward Israel in a single 24-hour period.

NVIDIA NemoClaw: Enterprise AI Agent Platform Debuts at GTC Tomorrow

NVIDIA will formally launch NemoClaw at Jensen Huang's GTC 2026 keynote tomorrow (March 16, 11am PT, SAP Center). NemoClaw is an open-source, hardware-agnostic enterprise AI agent platform built on NVIDIA's NeMo framework, Nemotron models, and NIM microservices — engineered for companies that need agents with enterprise-grade security, audit compliance (GDPR/HIPAA/CCPA), and RBAC. Critically, it runs on any hardware — NVIDIA, AMD, Intel — breaking from CUDA lock-in. The platform targets the enterprise gap left by consumer-focused OpenClaw. NVIDIA has discussed partnerships with Salesforce, Cisco, Google, Adobe, and CrowdStrike (per Wired; none confirmed). Also formal at tomorrow's keynote: Vera Rubin GPU (10x inference cost vs. current gen), Rubin CPX (1M token context), and Feynman first-look (TSMC A16 1.6nm). Livestream free at nvidia.com/gtc.

Oil + Hormuz: $200 Threat, IEA Release Failing

Iran doubled down on its $200/bbl warning. The IEA's announced 400M-barrel emergency release — the largest in history — has failed to push Brent below $100. Analysts broadly agree it cannot compensate for the effective closure of Hormuz, through which ~20% of global oil normally flows. 500+ tankers remain bottled. Iraq shut all oil terminal operations following tanker attacks near Basra. The US 30-day OFAC license for Russian oil at sea runs through April 11.

item.title: Lebanon: IDF Prepares Ground Assault, 14% Evacuated — IEA 400M-Barrel Release Fails to Move Oil — UN: 4M Displaced — Grok 5 (6T Params) Imminent — GTC TOMORROW: Vera Rubin 10x, Rubin CPX, Feynman
item.description:
Day 16 | March 15, 2026 — 06:00 UTC

LEBANON: IDF GROUND ASSAULT SIGNALED — 14% OF COUNTRY EVACUATED

Israeli PM Netanyahu's exact words at his March 12 press conference regarding Lebanon: "How will we do it? On the ground. With ground forces and other things." Defense Minister Katz instructed the IDF to prepare for expanded Lebanon operations after Hezbollah fired a heavy overnight barrage. Katz warned Lebanese President Aoun: "if your government cannot prevent Hezbollah from attacking, Israel would do it ourselves."

Scale of the displacement:

  • Norwegian Refugee Council: Israeli evacuation orders now cover ~1,470 km² — 14% of Lebanon's entire territory
  • UN peacekeeping (UNIFIL): Ground incursions and roadblocks by IDF forces reported in southern Lebanon
  • Lebanon death toll: ~800 killed, 850,000+ displaced
  • Hezbollah's political ally Amal Movement voted in favor of Lebanese cabinet decision to ban Hezbollah military activity — the first major fracture since 2005

IEA RECORD 400M-BARREL EMERGENCY RELEASE — MARKET UNIMPRESSED

The IEA's 32-member countries unanimously agreed (March 11) to release 400 million barrels of oil — the largest emergency stockpile release in the agency's 50-year history. The US is contributing 172 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve starting this week (120-day delivery timeline).

Market verdict: Brent stayed above $100. Analysis from Rapidan Energy Group: "IEA drawdowns can at best only offset a fraction of the roughly 15 million barrels per day net supply loss." Bob McNally: "Oil prices likely to keep rising until either a ceasefire or military degradation of Iran's attack capabilities allows tanker traffic to resume." Analysts flagging risk this crisis "could last for months instead of weeks."

UN: 4 MILLION DISPLACED; UAE ARRESTS 21 FOR FILMING STRIKES

The UN refugee agency reports up to 4 million people have been displaced inside Iran and Lebanon combined since February 28. Separately, the UAE arrested 21 people under its cybercrime laws for circulating videos of drone and missile activity online — a sign of Gulf state anxiety about the conflict's information environment.

BBC VERIFY: IRANIAN USV ATTACK AUTHENTICATED

BBC Verify authenticated footage of the Iranian unmanned surface vehicle (USV) attack on SAFESEA VISHNU off Basra (March 11): video shows the boat ramming the tanker, a massive explosion, and crew members celebrating. Indian authorities confirmed: "unmanned speed boat carrying explosives rammed into it, resulting in a major fire." 1 crew member died; 38 rescued. 3 crew from the MAYUREE NAREE remain missing in Hormuz. The USV program is now confirmed as a frontline offensive capability beyond the Strait.

AI: GROK 5 IMMINENT — 6 TRILLION PARAMETERS ON COLOSSUS 2

xAI's Grok 5 — the largest publicly announced AI model in history — is in final training stages on Colossus 2 (1GW supercluster, confirmed operational December 2025). Key specs:

  • 6 trillion parameters (Mixture-of-Experts) — roughly 3x GPT-5.4's estimated 1.8T
  • Musk: "10% and rising" probability of achieving human-level AGI
  • Public beta: estimated March–April 2026; full API Q2 2026
  • Native video understanding, real-time X data, 68M English posts/day as live training signal
  • Manifold prediction markets: 36% probability of release before May 1

The Grok 4.20 multi-agent series (4-agent/16-agent, released Feb-March) is widely interpreted as a public runway test for Grok 5's full multi-agent architecture. Colossus 2's 2GW expansion is another signal.

AI: GTC KEYNOTE TOMORROW — Vera Rubin 10x, Rubin CPX, Feynman

Jensen Huang keynote: Monday March 16, 11am PT (SAP Center, San Jose) — free livestream at nvidia.com. What's expected:

  • Vera Rubin: 10x inference cost reduction vs Blackwell; 4x fewer GPUs for MoE model training; first products H2 2026
  • Rubin CPX: Processes 1 million tokens in a single shot — "enough to read an entire codebase"
  • Feynman architecture: Built on TSMC's 1.6nm A16 process with silicon photonics integration — Jensen's "chip that will surprise the world"
  • OpenClaw + DGX Spark: 310K star agent platform demo; always-on personal AI computer

A 10x inference cost reduction means today's "expensive" AI use cases become next year's baseline infrastructure. The implications for autonomous coding, medical AI, and real-time military target analysis are significant across every domain touched by this war.

GTC coverage will follow Monday. Next update ~12:00 UTC March 15. Shabdis arrives Chabahar today (March 16).

item.title: Putin Offers Trump Iran Exit Ramp — IDF Prepares Lebanon Ground Expansion — Iranian USVs Hit Two Ships — Grok-4.20 Multi-Agent Beta (16 Agents) — Russian Oil Sanctions Waived
item.description:
Day 15 | March 14, 2026 — 18:00 UTC

DIPLOMACY: PUTIN OFFERS TRUMP IRAN EXIT RAMP

In a March 9 phone call — their first of 2026 — Putin proposed to Trump ideas for "a quick political and diplomatic end to the Iranian conflict," citing contacts with Gulf state leaders and the Iranian president (Kremlin aide Ushakov). Trump: "Putin wants to be helpful on Iran." March 10: Kremlin spokesman Peskov confirmed "many of these proposals are still on the table."

Context: Trump envoy Steve Witkoff told Russia to stop supplying intelligence to Iran (WaPo: Moscow giving Tehran US military asset locations including warships/aircraft). Peskov declined to comment — but confirmed Witkoff is "in constant contact with Russian interlocutors" on "the most sensitive issues."

Putin's dual play: publicly condemning the war while benefiting from oil price surge, offering mediation while giving Iran targeting data. The back-channel is real. Whether it produces anything depends on Iran accepting conditions.

IDF PREPARES LEBANON GROUND EXPANSION

Per ISW (March 12): Israeli Defense Minister Katz and PM Netanyahu instructed the IDF to prepare for "an expansion of IDF activity in Lebanon." Hezbollah simultaneously claimed 27 attacks on IDF in an 18-hour window — the highest since the war began. If Israel opens a major Lebanon ground operation, the war's geographic scope would significantly expand.

IRANIAN USVs HIT TWO SHIPS OFF BASRA

ISW (March 12): Suspected Iranian unmanned surface vessels (USVs) struck the US-owned SAFESEA VISHNU (Marshall Islands-flagged) and the ZEFYROS (Malta-flagged) off the coast of Basra, Iraq overnight March 11-12. Iran's USV program — first confirmed earlier in this conflict — is now an active threat beyond the Strait of Hormuz into Iraqi coastal waters.

PARCHIN NUCLEAR COMPLEX STRUCK

IDF struck the Taleghan 2 site at Iran's Parchin military complex (Tehran Province) on March 12. Taleghan 2 was previously used to test explosives needed to detonate a nuclear device. This confirms the campaign now includes direct targeting of Iran's nuclear weapons development infrastructure.

RUSSIAN OIL SANCTIONS — EUROPE PUSHES BACK

US Treasury Secretary Bessent's 30-day Russian oil sanctions waiver (announced March 13) is drawing sharp European resistance: German Chancellor Merz called it "wrong"; Macron said it "in no way" justified the relief; Canadian PM Carney wants Russia shadow fleet sanctions maintained. UK Foreign Secretary Cooper declined to criticize the US decision but called Russia and Iran a joint threat "hijacking the global economy." Average US fuel prices up 65 cents/gallon in a month; Brent remains ~$100.

AI: xAI GROK-4.20 MULTI-AGENT BETA — UP TO 16 PARALLEL AGENTS

xAI released Grok-4.20 Multi-Agent Beta on March 9 (available via OpenRouter, grok.com). The standout feature: reasoning effort levels directly control agent count:

  • Low/medium effort: 4 agents working in parallel
  • High/xhigh effort: 16 agents in parallel
  • 2M context window; $2/M input, $6/M output; multimodal

Also released March 9: Grok-4.20 Non-Reasoning and Grok-4.20 Reasoning as separate variants. All three accessible via API/OpenRouter. xAI's data moat: 68M English posts/day from X's firehose for real-time signal ingestion.

AI: OPENCLAW HITS 310K GITHUB STARS

OpenClaw v2026.3.12 shipped March 13 — the open-source always-on AI assistant framework now stands at 310,000 GitHub stars, making it one of the fastest-growing projects in history. This release brings:

  • Dashboard v2: modular overview, chat, config, agent, and session views + command palette + mobile tabs
  • GPT-5.4 fast mode + Claude fast mode (maps to Anthropic's service_tier API)
  • Kubernetes starter deploy path with raw manifests
  • Agent sessions_yield: orchestrators can end turns immediately, carrying hidden follow-up payload into next session
  • Security fixes: short-lived bootstrap tokens, disabled implicit workspace plugin auto-load

Next update ~06:00 UTC March 15. Shabdis ETA Chabahar tomorrow. GTC keynote Monday 11am PT.

item.title: BDA Update: 6,000 Targets Struck, 10K+ IRGC KIA/WIA, Missile Crews Deserting — Israel Hits Quds Day Rally — Microsoft Phi-4-Reasoning-Vision, GTC Mystery Chip
item.description:
Day 15 | March 14, 2026 — 12:00 UTC

BDA DEEP DIVE: CAMPAIGN TOTALS (Alma Research / CENTCOM)

Per CENTCOM data and Israeli military officials (Alma Research March 13 report):

  • 6,000 targets struck across Iran since February 28
  • 90+ Iranian vessels destroyed or damaged
  • Missile launcher arsenal: Israeli estimate — 160-190 ballistic missile launchers destroyed; ~200 more blocked/disabled; ~150 active launchers remaining. Bloomberg counters: ~60% destroyed, "held largely steady" — methodological dispute over how to count mobile vs. fixed launchers.
  • 10,000+ Iranian security and internal forces killed or wounded since Feb 28 (per Israeli military official, N12)
  • Missile crews deserting and refusing orders due to constant fear of being hunted — Iranian missile personnel cited as "terrified" by daily hunter-killer operations

24-HOUR STRIKE SUMMARY (Alma Research, Day 14)

Over the past 24 hours — more than 200 targets struck at 10+ sites:

  • Shiraz: Underground ballistic missile production and storage site; air force base; airport control tower
  • Tehran: Air defense array central base; weapons production and defense components facilities; Basij unit checkpoints
  • Ahvaz: IRGC Ground Forces HQ; central internal security HQ
  • + Bandar Abbas AF base, Khomein missile base, Bushehr, Karaj, Qom, Arak

ISRAEL STRIKES QUDS DAY RALLY IN CENTRAL TEHRAN

A large explosion rocked a central Tehran square during Iran's annual Quds Day rally on Friday March 13 — Israel had explicitly warned it would target the area. Thousands of protesters including senior officials were present. No casualties reported immediately. Iran's propaganda machine pressed on: "the regime's strength and survivability." The decision to warn and then strike a mass demonstration underscores the campaign's psychological dimension alongside military objectives.

LEBANON HUMANITARIAN CRISIS DEEPENS

AP (March 13): Lebanon death toll now ~800 killed, 850,000 displaced. Israel continues waves of strikes against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, Beqaa Valley, and Dahiyeh. Hezbollah's key political ally Amal Movement voted in favor of a Lebanese cabinet decision to ban Hezbollah's military activity — the first major fracture in the Amal-Hezbollah political alliance since 2005.

CENTCOM: "DECISIVE STEPS" VIDEO RELEASED

CENTCOM released new airstrike video Friday March 13, stating it is taking "decisive steps to neutralize Iran's power projection capabilities." The release is part of a sustained information operation alongside the kinetic campaign.

AI: MICROSOFT PHI-4-REASONING-VISION-15B — Open Weight, Thinks When It Needs To

Microsoft released Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B (March 4-5) — a 15B open-weight multimodal model with a standout feature: selective chain-of-thought reasoning. The model decides on its own when to reason and when to answer directly, based on task complexity. Key specs:

  • Trained on ~200B tokens — roughly 1/5 the data used by Qwen3-VL and Gemma3
  • ScreenSpot v2 (UI grounding): 88.2 — strong for computer-use agents
  • Three modes: Hybrid (model decides), Think (force reasoning), Nothink (skip it)
  • Available on Microsoft Foundry, HuggingFace, GitHub under permissive license

Designed as a perception layer for computer-use agents. Pushes the Pareto frontier: competitive with models requiring 10x the compute.

GTC PREVIEW: NVIDIA MYSTERY CHIP — N1X + SILICON PHOTONICS?

With Jensen Huang keynote 2 days away (March 16, 11am PT), speculation is building on the "chip that will surprise the world":

  • N1X AI PC Superchip: Rumored NVIDIA+MediaTek joint venture — Arm-based SoC with 20 custom cores + integrated GPU matching RTX 5070 performance. Would put NVIDIA directly into the laptop AI PC market vs. Apple and Qualcomm.
  • Silicon Photonics breakthrough: Supply chain leaks suggest a data-center networking advance that addresses the "power wall" as GPU clusters scale beyond current electrical interconnects.
  • Feynman architecture: First public architectural reveal expected — designed specifically for reasoning tasks and long-term memory in agentic AI systems.
  • Vera Rubin deep dive: 336B transistors, HBM4 memory, 3.3–5x FP4 improvement over Blackwell.

NVIDIA market cap at ~$4.6T entering the keynote. Free livestream at nvidia.com.

Next update ~18:00 UTC March 14. GTC keynote Monday 11am PT.

item.title: Iran Military Fractures: Artesh–IRGC Rift, Desertions, Supply Crisis — Netanyahu Veiled Kill Threat on Mojtaba — CENTCOM Destroys 30+ Minelayers
item.description:
Day 15 | March 14, 2026 — 06:00 UTC

IRAN MILITARY FRACTURES — Exclusive (Iran International)

Iran's armed forces are fracturing under combat strain. The Artesh (regular army) and IRGC are in open institutional conflict:

  • IRGC refusing to evacuate wounded Artesh soldiers to hospitals — citing "shortage of ambulances and blood supplies" — deepening resentment between the two forces
  • Frontline units operating with as few as 10 bullets per soldier; inadequate food and drinking water at multiple positions
  • Group desertions: soldiers leaving bases, seeking refuge in nearby towns
  • Reserve mobilization attempts met with resistance; morale collapse spreading even into IRGC missile units (comms equipment failures, food shortages)
  • Command prioritizing missile system components over basic soldier welfare

ISW caveat: Western media has not confirmed mass defections. Fracture is real but may not yet be operationally decisive.

NETANYAHU FIRST PRESS CONFERENCE — Veiled Kill Threat

Netanyahu's first presser since war began. Standing between two Israeli flags: "I wouldn't issue life insurance policies on any of the leaders of the terrorist organization... I don't intend to give an exact message here about what we are planning or what we are going to do." Said attacks are "creating conditions for the Iranian population to topple the government."

CENTCOM: 30+ MINELAYING SHIPS DESTROYED

CENTCOM confirmed destroying 30+ Iranian minelaying vessels and struck mine warehouses and manufacturing facilities across Iran. Strikes observed in 10 provinces in 24 hours. Iran retains ~80-90% mine-laying capacity — supply chain degradation is the new strategy.

KUWAIT AIRPORT HIT; TRUMP WARNS

Kuwait International Airport struck by multiple Iranian drones March 12 — material damage, air traffic briefly diverted, operations resumed. Citibank closed UAE branches. Trump Truth Social (early March 13): "Watch what happens to these deranged scumbags today."

HEZBOLLAH DRONE PIVOT CONFIRMED (ISW)

ISW: Hezbollah now relies more heavily on drones than rockets against IDF — a significant tactical shift. Hezbollah + IRGC simultaneous barrage March 12: 200 missiles at Israel, only 2 hit. Iraqi Shia militants also escalating drone/missile attacks on US interests.

IRAN CYBER: US HEALTHCARE TARGETED

Iranian-linked hackers struck a US healthcare company March 11 (ISW, March 12). Assessed as part of Iran's broader strategy to impose political pressure on Trump administration via civilian disruption — parallel to oil tanker and Hormuz tactics.

SHIP WATCH: SHABDIS ETA MARCH 16

Shabdis (IRISL, suspected sodium perchlorate / rocket fuel precursor) due at Chabahar March 16. Barzin en route to Bandar Abbas ~March 20. No US interdiction signaled. China did not delay departure (Carnegie's Isaac Kardon).

AI: McKINSEY "LILLI" BREACHED IN 2 HOURS

FT: Security startup CodeWall used an AI agent to access McKinsey's internal AI platform "Lilli" in under 2 hours — exposing 46.5M internal chat messages, tens of thousands of accounts, and hundreds of thousands of AI assistant interactions. A stark warning about rapidly deployed enterprise AI and its attack surface.

AI COMPANIES POUR $185M+ INTO 2026 MIDTERMS

WaPo: OpenAI- and Anthropic-backed groups directed $185M+ toward 2026 midterm races targeting AI policy candidates. In early Texas and North Carolina primaries, 19 of 20 AI-funded candidates won. Battle: federal vs. state-by-state AI regulation.

GTC PREVIEW: OPENCLAW + DGX SPARK (March 16, 11am PT)

NVIDIA spotlighting OpenClaw — "fastest-growing open source project in history" — an always-on agent framework deployable on DGX Spark (NVIDIA's personal AI computer). Jensen Huang keynote Monday 11am PT, SAP Center. Full Vera Rubin + Feynman chip reveal expected.

Next update ~12:00 UTC March 14. GTC keynote Monday 11am PT — live coverage to follow.

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Reportedly in Coma; Italy Quits Erbil; 2 Indians Killed in Oman; Vance-Trump Rift Widens; F1 Cancels Gulf Races
item.description:
MOJTABA KHAMENEI REPORTEDLY IN COMA, UNAWARE HE IS SUPREME LEADER. Multiple reports (i24NEWS, The Sun via Tehran source) say Iran's new Supreme Leader is hospitalized in intensive care at Sina University Hospital after sustaining serious injuries — including the reported loss of a leg — in a Feb. 28 airstrike. Source claims he is "unaware there is a war on and has no idea he is supreme leader." Iranian officials deny; his first "statement" was read by a state TV anchor with no camera appearance. Hegseth said Friday he is "likely disfigured." Raises deep questions about who is actually commanding Iran's war strategy — the IRGC's Mosaic Defence doctrine suggests pre-planned decentralized command regardless.

HEGSETH: FRIDAY WAS HIGHEST-STRIKE DAY OF WAR; 15,000+ TARGETS HIT. Defense Secretary said US-Israel campaign has now struck "well over 15,000 enemy targets" since Feb. 28 — more than 1,000 a day. Iran's drone attacks are down 95%, missiles down 90%. "Iran has no air defenses, Iran has no air force, Iran has no navy." Predicted "very soon, all of Iran's defense companies will be destroyed." On Hormuz: "exercising sheer desperation." Trump on Truth Social: "We have unparalleled firepower, unlimited ammunition, and plenty of time." And: "They've been killing innocent people all over the world for 47 years, and now I, as the 47th President of the United States of America, am killing them. What a great honor it is to do so!"

QUDS DAY TEHRAN STRIKE: ISRAEL BOMBED STATE RALLY AMID SENIOR OFFICIALS. A large explosion struck Ferdowsi Square in central Tehran during the annual Quds (Jerusalem) Day demonstration. Israel pre-warned on its Farsi-language X account it would "conduct operations" in the area. Security chief Ali Larijani, judiciary head Mohseni Ejehi, and other senior officials were present — visibly defying Israeli threats. Iranian state TV confirmed 1 killed; AP reported no immediate casualties. Dual signal: Iran holding public rally in defiance; Israel refusing to let it pass.

IRAN HITS GULF STATES — DAY 14 WAVE. Saudi Arabia intercepted 51 drones in a single morning, including a drone targeting Riyadh's Diplomatic Quarter (foreign embassies). Oman: 2 Indian nationals killed, 10 Indians among 11 injured in drone strike on Sohar's Al-Awahi Industrial Zone — India's MEA confirmed; significant given India held a diplomatic Hormuz carve-out. Dubai: intercept debris caused facade damage to a building in DIFC financial district; Abu Dhabi shelter-in-place orders issued. Iran is now on its 44th wave of "Operation True Promise 4."

ITALY WITHDRAWS TROOPS FROM ERBIL; NATO BASE HIT. Italy has begun pulling troops from its military base in Erbil, Iraq, after a missile struck the NATO-linked facility — the latest in a string of Iran-linked attacks on Erbil. UK troops at the same base shot down Iranian drones in a separate incident. The withdrawal marks a significant signal of NATO coalition erosion under sustained Iranian pressure on bases across Iraq.

JD VANCE "FEARS IRAN WAR DISASTER" AS 2028 ODDS CRATER. Vance privately fears the war "will be a catastrophe," per Politico/Daily Mail sourcing. Secretary of State Rubio and Hegseth have "basked in the glory" of Epic Fury while Vance faded to the background. Trump publicly admitted on stage that Vance was "less enthusiastic" about the strikes — first public acknowledgment of daylight within the administration. Vance is "walking a tightrope," having told Fox on March 2 that Trump won't allow a "multiyear conflict with no clear end in sight."

F1 CANCELS BAHRAIN AND SAUDI ARABIA GRANDS PRIX. Formula 1 confirmed the April 10-12 (Bahrain) and April 17-19 (Jeddah) races will be cancelled by end of this weekend, reducing the 2026 season to 22 races. A five-week gap opens between Japan (March 27-29) and Miami (May 1-3). Portimao and Imola mooted as replacements but too little lead time. Combined lost fees exceed $100 million.

GTC 2026 PREVIEW — JENSEN HUANG KEYNOTE MARCH 16. NVIDIA's GPU Technology Conference opens Monday in San Jose (March 16-19, SAP Center + Convention Center). Keynote 11am-1pm PT. Jensen has teased "a chip that will surprise the world." Confirmed: Vera Rubin formal deep-dive (H2 2026, 3.3x-5x performance over Blackwell, HBM4, TSMC A16 1.6nm), Blackwell Ultra production timeline. Expected: Feynman architecture first public mention. 30,000+ attendees from 190 countries; livestream free.

item.title: KC-135 All 6 Crew Confirmed Dead; Michigan Synagogue Suspect ID'd; CENTCOM Targets Mine Factories; ISW: Artesh-IRGC Fractures
item.description:
ALL 6 KC-135 CREW CONFIRMED DEAD. Gen. Dan Caine (Joint Chiefs): crashed "while crew was on a combat mission." Hegseth: "American heroes." Total US KIA now at least 14 (up from 8). A second KC-135 made emergency landing with significant tail damage. CENTCOM still attributes crash to non-hostile cause; Islamic Resistance in Iraq claims missile kill.

MICHIGAN SYNAGOGUE SUSPECT ID'D. Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, 41, Lebanese-born US citizen (naturalized 2016). Family — niece, nephew, two others — reportedly killed in Israeli airstrike on Mashghara, Lebanon, March 5. FBI investigating as "targeted act of violence against Jewish community." Trump on Truth Social: "Watch what happens to these deranged scumbags today."

CENTCOM: 30+ MINE-LAYING SHIPS DESTROYED. Now targeting mine warehouses and factories across Iran — escalation from vessel interdiction to supply-chain strikes. Iran retains ~80–90% small-boat mine-laying capacity; Hormuz de-mining still estimated weeks to months.

ISW: ARTESH-IRGC FRACTURES DEEPENING. Supply shortages fracturing coordination between Iranian conventional military (Artesh) and IRGC. Hezbollah shifting from rockets to drones — new operational tactic reducing interceptable salvos. Iranian strikes hit 10 provinces March 12. Pezeshkian signals willingness to de-escalate under Iran's 3 conditions — directly contradicting Mojtaba's maximalist stance, a visible internal fracture.

ENERGY AND MARKETS. Oil back above $100/bbl on new strikes. Germany publicly criticizes US Russian oil sanctions waiver: "filling Putin's war chest while we fight Iran." US Q4 2025 GDP revised to +0.7% annualized — worse than estimated, amplifying recession fears as war drags on.

channel.title: Serenity News Briefing
channel.description: Curated news briefings: Iran/ME conflict, AI/ML, fintech, NYC politics. Updated every 12 hours.

item.title: Iran war Day 13: Michigan synagogue attack as markets crash; KC-135 crew fate unknown; IEA warns of largest oil disruption in history
item.description:
US DOMESTIC TERROR: A suspect drove a truck into Temple Israel synagogue in West Bloomfield, Michigan — one of the largest Reform synagogues in the United States — on Thursday afternoon. Security officers opened fire on the attacker; the vehicle breached the building, drove down a hallway, and stopped inside where a body was found. Something ignited in the vehicle, causing a fire inside the building. The Oakland County Sheriff said the suspect is dead. FBI Director Kash Patel arrived on scene, calling it "an apparent vehicle ramming and active shooter situation." FBI Special Agent-in-Charge Jennifer Runyan called it "a targeted act of violence against the Jewish community." The FBI is simultaneously investigating a second possible terror attack inside the US. The attack comes as the Iran-US war stokes antisemitic tensions globally.

KC-135 CREW STILL UNKNOWN: At least 5 crew members were aboard the KC-135 Stratotanker that crashed in western Iraq late March 12 during Operation Epic Fury. The Islamic Resistance in Iraq — an umbrella group of Iran-backed factions — claims it downed the aircraft with a missile and that all crew were killed. US Central Command maintains the crash was "not due to hostile fire or friendly fire" and says rescue and recovery efforts are ongoing. A second KC-135 that declared an in-flight emergency and landed from Israel was photographed missing a significant portion of its tail. No crew fatalities have been officially confirmed. This is the 4th US crewed aircraft lost in the conflict.

MARKETS IN FREEFALL: The Dow Jones fell 740 points Thursday, pushing US stocks to new 2026 lows. The NASDAQ declined. Asian markets followed: Nikkei -2%, Kospi -3%. Brent crude spiked +9.2% Thursday (topping $100 again) before easing slightly; WTI near $96. The IEA issued a stark warning: this is the largest oil supply disruption in the history of the global oil market — surpassing the 1970s oil shocks. The IEA's coordinated 400M barrel strategic reserve release (US contribution: 172M barrels) is underway but Goldman Sachs estimates it covers only ~12 days of the 15.4M bbl/day Hormuz disruption.

US INTELLIGENCE CONTRADICTS NETANYAHU: US intelligence sources told Reuters that the Iranian government is not at risk of collapse — directly contradicting Netanyahu's regime-change framing at his March 12 press conference, where he claimed Israeli strikes had killed "top Iranian nuclear scientists" and told Iranians "the moment for a new path of freedom is approaching." Trump said he thinks Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei "thinks he's going to win" — suggesting the US president does not share Netanyahu's optimism. Trump added: "Preventing Iran from having nuclear weapons is of far greater interest and importance to me than the cost of oil."

MOJTABA THREATENS "OTHER FRONTS": In his first public statement (March 12), Iran's new Supreme Leader threatened that "studies have been conducted into opening other fronts where the enemy has little experience and would be highly vulnerable." The statement also reiterated that Hormuz will stay closed as "a tool to pressure the enemy." Iran's Supreme National Security Council Secretary Ali Larijani separately vowed Iran "will not relent until making you sorry for this grave miscalculation."

UK CONFIRMS HORMUZ MINES: The United Kingdom has confirmed Iran has "likely" started laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, further reducing prospects for safe transit. De-mining operations are expected to take weeks or months. Bessent says the US Navy will escort tankers "as soon as militarily possible"; Energy Secretary Wright: "simply not ready." Navy escorts possibly by end of March (CNBC TV18).

ALSO: Hezbollah fired rocket salvos into northern Israel overnight March 12-13; Zarzir missile strike wounded 30 near Haifa. IDF's Golani Brigade redeployed from Gaza to Northern Command alongside Divisions 36 and 91 — ground escalation signal. Hezbollah retains 1,000+ long-range missiles (JPost). Lebanon: ~700 killed, 820K+ displaced.

item.title: Iran war Day 13: US opens Russian oil spigot as Iran threatens $200/bbl; IDF reinforces Lebanon front
item.description:
ECONOMIC WAR ESCALATING: US Treasury issued a 30-day OFAC waiver allowing all countries (not just India) to purchase Russian oil stranded at sea — vessels loaded on or before March 12 — through April 11. Treasury Secretary Bessent: "alleviate pressure caused by Iran's attempt to take global energy hostage." Brent crude briefly hit $99.75, falling below $100 for the first time since the war began (after closing at $101.29 Wednesday). The US contributed 172M barrels from its Strategic Petroleum Reserve to the IEA's coordinated 400M barrel release — Goldman Sachs estimates it covers only ~12 days of the 15.4M bbl/day disruption.

IRAN FIRES BACK: Military command spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaqari: "Get ready for oil at $200 a barrel." Zolfaqari also threatened attacks on banks doing business with the US or Israel, warning people within 1,000 meters to evacuate. The US government is sending mixed signals on Hormuz escorts: Bessent told Sky News the Navy will escort tankers "as soon as militarily possible," while Energy Secretary Chris Wright said the military is "simply not ready — all assets are focused on destroying Iran's offensive capabilities."

IDF REINFORCING NORTH: Israel moved the Golani Brigade from Gaza to Northern Command, alongside Divisions 36 and 91 already operating deeper into southern Lebanon than originally planned. IDF is targeting 1,000 strikes inside Lebanon. Intelligence assessments show Hezbollah retains 1,000+ long-range missiles despite sustained strikes. Overnight: Hezbollah rocket salvos hit northern Israel; a missile strike on Zarzir (near the Lebanese border, ~100km north of Jerusalem) wounded 30 people (Magen David Adom).

KC-135 CREW STILL UNKNOWN: The four-person crew of the KC-135 Stratotanker that crashed in western Iraq during a mid-air collision late March 12 remains unaccounted for. The aircraft has no ejection seats; rescue operations are continuing. This was the 4th US aircraft lost in Operation Epic Fury.

PENTAGON ECONOMIC DEFENSE UNIT: (Semafor scoop, March 11) The Pentagon is building a 30-person team to deploy $200B over three years to counter China's "gray zone" economic coercion. Search firm Heidrick & Struggles is recruiting from Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, JPMorgan, and Bank of America for 2-3 year secondments. Hegseth declined to comment.

ALSO: UK troops in Iraq shot down two Iranian drones after their base came under attack (The Independent). Iran's Supreme National Security Council Secretary Ali Larijani vowed Iran "will not relent until making you sorry for this grave miscalculation." Trump says the war is going "well."

item.title: Day 13: KC-135 Crashes in Iraq; Iran Cyberattack Hits US Hospitals; Hezbollah 200+ Rockets; Navy Abandons Hormuz Escort Plan
item.description:
KC-135 STRATOTANKER DOWN IN WESTERN IRAQ — CREW STATUS UNKNOWN

A US Air Force KC-135 Stratotanker crashed in western Iraq late Thursday during Operation Epic Fury. CENTCOM confirmed a mid-air incident involving two KC-135s; one went down, the other diverted safely to Ben Gurion Airport. "Not due to hostile or friendly fire," CENTCOM said, with rescue efforts ongoing. KC-135s carry crews of 6 with no ejection seats. This is the fourth US aircraft lost in the war and the first KC-135 crash since 2013. [Breaking Defense | The War Zone]

IRAN CYBER WAR HITS US HOSPITALS: STRYKER ATTACK DISRUPTS EMERGENCY EMS

Iran-linked hacking group Handala wiped 200,000+ systems at medical device giant Stryker (56K employees, 61 countries), seizing 50TB of data in apparent retaliation for the Minab school strike (168 killed). Stryker filed an SEC 8-K disclosing a "global network disruption to our Microsoft environment." The attack knocked out LifeNet — an EMS ECG transmission system used by first responders — across most of Maryland. Cybersecurity experts say Handala's targeting and tactics are "consistent with Iranian state actors." Stryker shares fell 3.6%. [Al Jazeera | Reuters]

HEZBOLLAH 200+ ROCKETS; ISRAEL EXPANDS LEBANON CAMPAIGN

Hezbollah fired 200+ rockets and missiles into northern Israel overnight March 12-13 — the largest single barrage since Day 1 — sending millions to shelters and drawing direct hits in Upper Galilee. IRGC-Hezbollah joint missile operation confirmed. Israel announced it is preparing to expand its ground incursion into southern Lebanon. In a striking break, Lebanese President Aoun publicly denounced Hezbollah and called for direct Lebanon-Israel talks. Lebanon toll: ~820,000 displaced, ~700 killed. [Bloomberg]

US NAVY: HORMUZ ESCORTS "TOO DANGEROUS — FOR NOW"

Behind closed doors, US Navy commanders told officials that actively escorting commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz is too dangerous at current threat levels, per gCaptain. 19 commercial ships struck since Feb. 28; only 5 tankers departed the Gulf in 24 hours. Trump pivoted Thursday from his "drill baby drill" posture: "When oil prices go up, we make a lot of money" — framing the crisis as a US revenue windfall. Brent settled at $100.46. S&P 500 fell 1.5%, Dow -739 pts. [gCaptain | AP]

Day 13 war summary: 4 US aircraft lost total; ~140 US wounded (8 severely); 8 KIA; Iran 1,348+ killed, 3.2M displaced; 19 commercial vessels struck; Hormuz 90%+ shut. No ceasefire path — Mojtaba vows Hormuz stays closed. NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super (120B/12B active, 5x throughput, open weights) launched March 11 for agentic AI.

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Breaks Silence: Hormuz Must Stay Closed, US Bases Will Be Attacked — Day 13
item.description: Iran's newly appointed Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei (56) issued his first public statement since taking power March 9 — read by a state TV anchor, with no camera appearance, consistent with Israeli intelligence assessments that he was wounded in the February 28 opening strikes. The statement was maximalist and escalatory: (1) "The leverage of closing the Strait of Hormuz must continue to be used." (2) All US military bases in the Middle East "should be shut down immediately — or they will be attacked." (3) Gulf states should evict US forces, calling US protection "nothing more than a lie." (4) Called on Houthi forces in Yemen and Iraqi militias to "help the Islamic revolution." The statement signals leadership continuity and a hardening war posture, ruling out any imminent ceasefire under pressure. Separately: Iran's UN Ambassador reported at least 1,348 Iranian civilians killed; UNHCR counts 3.2 million displaced; UNICEF says 1,100 children among casualties. US forces have now struck 28 Iranian mine-laying vessels (up from 16 on March 11). Three more commercial vessels were hit in the Strait on March 12, bringing the total to 14 struck since February 28. Hormuz remains effectively closed to oil traffic. Sources: AP, NBC News, Al Jazeera, CNBC, UNHCR, UNICEF — March 12, 2026.

item.title: Netanyahu: Iranian Nuclear Scientists Killed; IDF Launches New "Wide-Scale Wave" on Tehran; Brent Settles at $100.46
item.description:
Netanyahu Evening Press Conference (~20:00 UTC): Israeli PM Netanyahu announced that Israeli strikes have killed top Iranian nuclear scientists — a significant escalation in declared targeting objectives. He called Mojtaba Khamenei "a puppet of the Revolutionary Guards" who "cannot appear in public," addressing the Iranian people directly: "The moment for a new path of freedom is approaching." Netanyahu's remarks directly responded to Khamenei's first wartime statement earlier today.

IDF Launches New "Wide-Scale Wave" — New Phase Declared: The IDF announced a new wide-scale wave of strikes targeting infrastructure across Tehran Thursday evening (Israeli time). The IDF stated the campaign was entering a new phase targeting regime infrastructure broadly — a significant doctrinal shift from military/nuclear targeting toward civilian infrastructure pressure.

Brent Settles Above $100 for First Time: Brent crude closed at $100.46/barrel (+9.2% on the day) — the first confirmed settlement above $100 since the conflict began. WTI settled near $96. Iraq's General Company for Ports confirmed all port operations halted following Iranian tanker attacks in Iraqi territorial waters. IEA's record 400M barrel release failed to calm markets: analysts note the release can inject only 1.2-4M bbl/day vs. 11-16M bbl/day lost to the Hormuz closure. No US Treasury futures market intervention has been announced.

Witkoff/Ceasefire: No change in diplomatic impasse. Iran has formally rejected two Witkoff ceasefire messages. Iranian officials tell The Guardian they are "not losing the war" and intend to raise costs until Trump backs off.

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Issues First Wartime Statement: Hormuz Stays Closed, New Fronts Threatened; Anthropic Files DC Circuit Emergency Stay
item.description:
Mojtaba Khamenei — First Wartime Statement (~13:26 UTC Mar 12): Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first public statement since taking power, read on Iranian state television (he did not appear on camera; Israeli intelligence assesses he was wounded in the war's opening strikes). Key signals:
(1) Hormuz blockade continues — closed as "leverage" and will remain so;
(2) Regional countries hosting US bases warned: "close them or face attack";
(3) Iran is "studying new fronts where the enemy has little experience and will be extremely vulnerable" — will activate if war continues;
(4) Thanked Houthis, Hezbollah, and Iraqi resistance factions by name;
(5) Vowed to avenge "martyrs" including Minab school victims (170+ killed).
This statement forecloses near-term ceasefire talks and signals continuity of IRGC-directed strategy under new leadership. No confirmed proof of life — only a photo shown alongside the statement.

Iran Rejects Witkoff Twice; $50B+ Supplemental Request Expected: Iran has formally spurned at least two ceasefire messages from Trump envoy Steve Witkoff. Iran's FM Araghchi: even if the US declared victory and offered to stop strikes, Iran might continue the conflict to maintain economic pressure. Congress is furious at lack of briefing detail; a $50B+ supplemental defense funding request from the White House is expected imminently.

Anthropic Files DC Circuit Emergency Stay: Anthropic filed a new emergency stay request with the US Court of Appeals for the DC Circuit (separate from its California federal lawsuit), seeking to pause the Pentagon's supply-chain risk designation. Anthropic estimates "hundreds of millions or even multiple billions" in 2026 revenue at risk; 100+ enterprise customers have already raised concerns. A leaked Pentagon internal memo (March 6, signed by CIO Kirsten Davies) reveals the DoD quietly told its own senior leaders Anthropic tools could be exempted if "mission-critical" — suggesting how difficult the ban is to actually implement.

item.title: Day 14: Iraq Oil Ports Shut After Tanker Attacks in Iraqi Waters; Brent Crosses $100; Iran Hits Dubai Airport + Oman Salalah; UN Passes Resolution
item.description:
Iraq Oil Ports Shut — New Geographic Escalation: Iran attacked two fuel tankers near Iraq's Khor Al Zubair port using suicide boats, igniting both vessels and killing at least one Indian national (US-owned, Marshall Islands-flagged Safesea Vishnu; 38 crew rescued). Iraq shut down its oil export ports in response. First time the maritime campaign has reached Iraqi territorial waters — a major new front.

Iran Strikes Dubai International Airport + Oman's Port of Salalah: Drone/missile strikes disrupted Dubai International Airport (world's busiest by passenger volume). Separately, Iranian drones struck fuel storage tanks at Oman's Port of Salalah, sparking fires. Qatar also intercepted Iranian missiles and drones. Iran denied the Salalah attack. Pattern signals Iran is deliberately widening its campaign to neutral-country infrastructure, breaking prior implicit limits.

Brent Crosses $100/bbl — IEA Release Failing: Brent crude surged past $100 (up 9% overnight; WTI $95) as the Iraq port closure overwhelmed the IEA's record 400M-barrel release. IEA acknowledged the release is "palliative" — barrels take weeks to reach markets. US SPR drawdown (172M bbl US contribution) begins next week. US Treasury reportedly weighing intervention in oil futures — an extraordinary step. Asian equities fell broadly.

UN Security Council Passes Resolution: UNSC voted to demand Iran halt strikes on Gulf neighbors. Russia and China did not veto — a notable development.

Trump: "We've Won" — But War Continues: At a Kentucky rally (March 11 ET), Trump declared "we've won" while insisting the US must "finish the job." Claimed 58 Iranian naval ships destroyed and Iran "virtually destroyed." No ceasefire imminent — Iran's 3 conditions (rights recognition, reparations, binding guarantees) remain unmet.

item.title: Day 13: FBI West Coast Drone Alert, $11.3B War Cost Disclosed, Kuwait Troop Injuries Severe; Microsoft + Military Back Anthropic; NVIDIA Drops Nemotron 3 Super
item.description:
Iran War — Day 13 Overnight (04:00 UTC Mar 12):
FBI West Coast Alert: The FBI issued an alert to West Coast law enforcement warning of a possible Iranian retaliatory drone strike launched from an unidentified vessel offshore — first continental-US threat advisory of the conflict.

War Cost Disclosed: A closed-door Senate briefing revealed the US has spent $11.3 billion in the first six days of combat, a figure that tracks with earlier $890M–$1B/day estimates but now confirmed by classified briefing.

Kuwait Injuries More Severe Than Disclosed: CBS News confirmed that "dozens" of US troops sustained serious injuries from the Kuwait attack, including brain trauma, shrapnel wounds, and burns — the first substantive US casualty disclosure of the war. The Pentagon had previously characterized injuries as minor.

Iran: "Long-Term War of Attrition": Tehran formally stated its intention to wage a "long-term war of attrition that will destroy the entire American economy," and separately threatened to begin targeting US- and Israeli-linked bank branches across the Middle East. Ceasefire rejection remains firm.

Trump: "We're not stopping till the job is done. It will end soon — whenever I decide it should." White House still unable to articulate a clear victory condition.

Anthropic vs. Pentagon — Microsoft and 22 Retired Military Leaders File Amicus Briefs: Microsoft filed a legal challenge in San Francisco federal court asking a judge to block Hegseth's supply chain risk designation of Anthropic. Separately, 22 retired senior military officials (former service secretaries, former Coast Guard commandant) filed their own brief alleging Hegseth "abused government authority for retribution." This follows last week's OpenAI/Google employee brief (~900 signatories) — but this is corporate/institutional weight in the fight. Anthropic disclosed $5B+ all-time revenue and warned of billions in client losses.

NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super (Pre-GTC Drop): NVIDIA released a significant open model ahead of GTC: Nemotron 3 Super — 120B total / 12B active parameters, hybrid Mamba-Transformer MoE architecture, 1M token native context, 5x throughput over previous Nemotron Super. Open weights. Already integrated by Perplexity, Palantir, Siemens, CodeRabbit, Factory. Positioned specifically for multi-agent workflows with "context explosion" and "thinking tax" optimizations. GTC keynote still March 16 (SAP Center, 11 AM PDT) — Jensen Huang teased "a chip that will surprise the world."

item.title: Iran Formalizes 3 Conditions to End War: Rights Recognition, Reparations, Binding Guarantees — OpenAI and Google Workers File Amicus Brief Defending Anthropic vs. Pentagon
item.description:
Iran's 3 Formal Conditions to End War

Iranian President Pezeshkian has publicly formalized Tehran's conditions for ending the war, in statements to the leaders of Russia and Pakistan. Iran now requires three things before any cessation of hostilities: (1) Recognition of Iran's legitimate rights — including its right to civilian nuclear technology and regional influence; (2) Payment of reparations for damages inflicted by US and Israeli strikes; (3) Binding international guarantees against future aggression, from parties beyond the US-Israel axis. The reparations demand is new — Tehran had previously only demanded security guarantees. This positions Iran well beyond what Washington or Jerusalem could politically accept, and effectively rules out any near-term ceasefire. Foreign Minister Araghchi separately told Hindustan Times that Iran "cannot trust" any commitment from the US, citing the violated June 2025 ceasefire. Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf had already rejected negotiations outright. The three-condition formula suggests Iran is positioning for a drawn-out conflict, betting on oil market pain forcing Western political capitulation.

OpenAI and Google Workers Back Anthropic in Pentagon Lawsuit

More than 30 employees from OpenAI and Google DeepMind filed an amicus curiae legal brief supporting Anthropic's lawsuit against the US Department of Defense (March 9-10). Signatories include Jeff Dean, Google's chief scientist — a notable defection from a direct competitor. The brief argues the Pentagon's designation of Anthropic as a "supply chain risk" (a label normally reserved for foreign adversaries like China) was "an improper and arbitrary use of power that has serious ramifications for our industry." The brief specifically endorses Anthropic's "red lines" — refusing to enable mass domestic surveillance or fully autonomous lethal weapons — as "legitimate and widely recognised within our scientific community." Separately, nearly 900 employees at Google and OpenAI signed an open letter calling on their bosses to block DOD use of AI for those scenarios. The industry-wide pushback creates an unusual dynamic: companies competing fiercely for the same talent and customers are uniting against a federal designation that any of them could face next. The Pentagon signed an emergency OpenAI deal immediately after blacklisting Anthropic; OpenAI employees protested that move internally.

item.title: CNN Exclusive: Russia Transferring Ukraine Drone Swarm Tactics to Iran — Shahed "Unexpectedly Successful" Against Gulf Air Defenses; Australia Closes Gulf Embassies; Saudi Aramco Pipeline at Capacity
item.description:
Russia Transfers Ukraine Drone Swarm Tactics to Iran (CNN Exclusive)

CNN reports a Western intelligence official confirms Russia is providing Iran with specific advanced drone tactics drawn from its war in Ukraine — a significant escalation beyond the general targeting intelligence previously reported. Iran's Shahed drones, designed by Tehran but mass-produced in Russia, have been "unexpectedly successful" at penetrating Gulf state air defenses. Russian advisors are sharing UAS strategies developed in Ukraine: simultaneous waves, course-changing patterns, and mass saturation attacks (Ukraine faced 1,000+ drones in single nights). Ukraine President Zelensky confirmed on X: "Russia has started supporting the Iranian regime with drones. It will definitely help with missiles, and is also helping with air defense." Russia has denied the assistance. The US has also separately confirmed that Russian satellites are feeding Iran real-time overhead imagery. Taken together, Russian support has moved from passive intelligence-sharing to active tactical coaching — fundamentally improving Iran's ability to exhaust US/Gulf air defense magazines.

Australia Closes Gulf Embassies + Consulate

Australia has shuttered two embassies and one consulate across Gulf states, citing security threats from the ongoing conflict. The closures signal growing Western nervousness about the safety of diplomatic personnel as Iran escalates targeting to "US/Israeli-linked" sites and financial institutions. Australia joins the UK (RAF Akrotiri/Dhekelia strikes) and Norway (Oslo US Embassy device) in suffering spillover security threats.

Saudi Aramco East-West Pipeline Approaching Capacity

Saudi Aramco warned that its East-West pipeline — the primary alternative to Hormuz for Red Sea shipments — is rapidly approaching its full capacity of ~5M bbl/day. If Hormuz remains disrupted and the fallback pipeline maxes out, global oil supply chains face a hard ceiling with no remaining bypass. Saudi oil minister met IEA counterparts on the sidelines of the G7 energy coordination session. Goldman Sachs reiterated its 12-day coverage analysis: even with the record IEA 400M barrel release, the math doesn't work if Hormuz stays blocked long-term.

Day 12 Spillover: Thai Navy, Iran Football Team, Baalbek

Thai navy responded to an attack on a bulk carrier transiting the Strait of Hormuz — the first sign of third-country naval engagement in the conflict zone. Iran's football federation head raised doubts about Iran's participation in the June 2026 World Cup after several members of the Iranian women's soccer team sought asylum in Australia. Israeli airstrikes killed 10 in Baalbek, Lebanon; French President Macron stated Israel "must respect Lebanese sovereignty." US Senator Thom Tillis publicly stated the US "shouldn't gloss over" the Minab school strike — a rare acknowledgment of potential US culpability by a Republican senator. Dubai International Airport was struck again; traffic remains operational.

item.title: IEA Executes Record 400M Barrel Release; Markets Unimpressed as Oil Rises — Anthropic Institute Launched; Musk Unveils Tesla-xAI "Macrohard"
item.description:
IEA: Record 400M Barrels, Markets Not Convinced

The IEA formally executed its largest-ever emergency oil release on Wednesday — 32 member countries unanimously agreed to release 400 million barrels from strategic reserves to address the Iran war supply disruption. IEA Executive Director Fatih Birol: "The oil market challenges we are facing are unprecedented in scale." The release covers ~26 days of IEA member consumption but markets were unimpressed — oil prices rose anyway, as analysts noted the release only covers ~12 days of the estimated 15.4M bbl/day Gulf export disruption (per Goldman Sachs). Trump endorsed the IEA move. France's Macron chaired a simultaneous G7 leaders call on energy stability. Iran continues to hold ~80–90% of its mine-laying capacity even after CENTCOM destroyed 16 mine-laying vessels earlier this week.

Trump: "Practically Nothing Left to Target" — Iran Still Refuses Ceasefire

Trump told Axios that the war would end "soon" because there was "practically nothing left to target" in Iran — while simultaneously vowing to hit Iran "twenty times harder" if Hormuz oil flows are further disrupted. Iran's FM Araghchi appeared on NBC Meet the Press to formally reject any ceasefire, demanding a permanent resolution rather than a short-term truce. Araghchi cited violations of the June 2025 ceasefire as proof that US/Israeli promises are worthless. Iran parliament speaker Ghalibaf also fully rejected negotiations. Qatar confirmed back-channel diplomatic contacts remain open. No ceasefire imminent.

Anthropic Institute: New Think Tank Launched Amid Pentagon Blacklist Fight

Anthropic announced the Anthropic Institute on Wednesday — a new internal research body led by co-founder Jack Clark (new title: Head of Public Benefit). The Institute merges three existing Anthropic teams: Frontier Red Team, Societal Impacts, and Economic Research. Focus: studying AI's large-scale effects on jobs, economies, governance, and potential recursive self-improvement. Launch coincides with Anthropic's active lawsuit against the Trump administration over its Pentagon blacklist designation. Clark: "The pace of AI progress isn't slowing itself down for external events, and neither are we." New hires include economist Anton Korinek (transformative AI/economic activity) and Zoë Hitzig (linking economics with AI training). Anthropic also opening planned DC office and expanding public policy team under new lead Sarah Heck.

Musk Unveils Tesla-xAI "Macrohard" / Digital Optimus

Elon Musk announced a joint Tesla-xAI project called "Macrohard" (also "Digital Optimus") — a computer-controlling AI agent system pairing xAI's Grok as high-level "navigator" with a Tesla-built AI that processes real-time screen video and keyboard/mouse actions. Musk claimed the system can "emulate the function of entire companies" and runs on Tesla's $650 AI4 chip paired with xAI's Nvidia-based cloud hardware. The announcement directly contradicts Musk's September 2024 statement that Tesla had "no need to license anything from xAI." Tesla shareholders actively suing Musk for breach of fiduciary duty over xAI's founding. SpaceX acquired xAI last month ($1T SpaceX + $250B xAI valuation). Claude Cowork — now integrated into Microsoft Copilot via "Copilot Cowork" — is cited as the competitive trigger for Musk's move.

item.title: Israel launches "wide-scale wave" of strikes on Tehran and Beirut simultaneously
item.description: Israel has announced a "wide-scale wave" of strikes targeting both Tehran and Beirut simultaneously. The IDF says it is targeting Hezbollah infrastructure and Iranian regime targets. The simultaneous dual-city operation represents a significant escalation — the first time Israel has struck both Iranian and Lebanese capitals in the same wave during the current conflict. Lebanon has seen 486 killed and 700,000 displaced since the war began; IDF had already issued evacuation orders for Tyre and Sidon ahead of this wave.

item.title: NVIDIA GTC 2026 Preview (March 16): Feynman First Reveal, Vera Rubin Deep Dive — Jensen Huang Teases "A Chip That Will Surprise the World"; Qatar Confirms Iran Diplomatic Channels Remain Open
item.description: NVIDIA's GTC 2026 conference opens in five days (March 16–19, San Jose). Jensen Huang's keynote is confirmed for Monday March 16 at 8:00am PDT (free virtual livestream). Key expected announcements: (1) Vera Rubin deep dive — full engineering specs for NVIDIA's next-gen platform; Vera CPU replaces Grace, paired with HBM4; 10x inference token cost reduction vs. Blackwell, 4x fewer GPUs for MoE training; H2 2026 production. (2) Feynman first reveal — Jensen explicitly said "at GTC 2026, we'll unveil a chip that will surprise the world"; Feynman targets TSMC 1.6nm A16 with silicon photonics; agentic AI workloads; N1X Arm-based CPUs competing with Intel/Qualcomm in laptops and AI hardware. (3) Physical AI factory infrastructure thesis — NVIDIA shifting from pure compute sales to integrated AI factory systems. Context: NVIDIA's Q4 FY2026 was record $68.1B (+73% YoY); Q1 FY2027 guided at $78B. DeepSeek V4 and Huawei Ascend-native Chinese models have raised questions about NVIDIA's long-term moat. Also: Qatar's foreign ministry spokesperson confirmed March 10 that communication channels between Qatar and Iran "are not cut," keeping alive Qatar's role as chief mediator even as Iran maintains its hardline public ceasefire position.

item.title: Iran Growing More Defiant, Not Less: Demands Binding No-Attack Guarantee; Araghchi Warns Tehran May Continue War Even If US Stops; Witkoff: "I Don't Know" How War Ends
item.description: Guardian diplomatic editor Patrick Wintour reports Iran has become MORE defiant since the war began, not less. Iran's Deputy FM Gharibabadi stated: "If a ceasefire is to be established or the war stopped there must be a guarantee that aggressive actions against Iran will not be repeated. Otherwise if another attack occurs after a few months such a ceasefire would be meaningless." More striking: FM Araghchi implied that even a unilateral US declaration of victory and halt in attacks would NOT end the conflict — Iran might continue fighting in some form. Iran believes the war cannot stop until Tehran has demonstrated to Trump that the economic, political, and military cost is so high it cannot be repeated. This marks a major hardening from Iran's earlier position (which was security guarantees) to an explicit demand for binding written guarantees AND continuation leverage. US Special Envoy Witkoff told CNBC on March 10: "I don't know" how the war ends, adding only "President Trump is the wrong guy to go up against." Witkoff's Israel trip for ceasefire coordination was cancelled and rescheduled to "next week." Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf separately confirmed Iran "is not seeking a ceasefire." Qatar, Turkey, Oman, and UN are actively mediating; China and Russia have contacted Tehran. The Guardian analysis: Iran senses it is NOT losing the war and reads US political pressure (Vance rift, Trump's "not this week" language) as signs Washington may blink first.

item.title: UNSC Votes on GCC Resolution Demanding Iran Halt Arab State Attacks; Iran Fires 37th Wave at Camp Arifjan; Goldman Sachs: IEA Release Only Covers 12 Days of Gulf Disruption
item.description: The UN Security Council voted Wednesday afternoon on a GCC-sponsored resolution condemning Iran's missile and drone attacks against Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Jordan, and demanding an immediate halt. Iran's UN envoy Amir Saeid Iravani warned adoption would "reward aggressors" and be a "stain on the credibility of the Council." Russia and China are expected to veto or block the measure. Separately, Iran's IRGC declared its 37th wave of attacks, firing 4 missiles at US Middle East HQ including 2 targeting Camp Arifjan (Kuwait); Kuwait's National Guard downed 8 drones. Qatar intercepted a fresh missile attack over Doha.
